mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is an open-source cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it suitable for production environments requiring reliable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Ceph and the need for careful resource management.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook from the official source.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-ceph' namespace, which is necessary for managing Ceph storage.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-ceph

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) that Rook manages.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Ceph Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-ceph namespace to verify they are running.
> Executes a command in the Rook Ceph tools pod to check the Ceph cluster status.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Ceph cluster and all associated data, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph

kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the Rook operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create pool'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the Ceph cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and configuration in the cluster YAML file.

troubleshooting.title: Pod in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This warning indicates that there are issues with the cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph health detail' to get more information.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to Ceph dashboard
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is exposed correctly and that network policies allow access.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance of the Ceph cluster, added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state and 'ceph status' indicating 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster is set up and accessible.
Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that kubectl and helm are installed.
